mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present values of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer spending strength, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regression or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher short-term corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary supply bottlenecks, yielding negative returns in > 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.5 percent annually, the Federal Reserve initiates aggressive rate hikes that compress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines by > 2% Sharpe ratio or interaction coefficient turns negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which increases corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions erase predictive power, causing Rank IC to fall below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial output and acceleration in consumer prices signals stagflationary pressures that erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: Market prices in stagflation risks prematurely, resulting in positive forward returns in > 60% of trigger instances.
